source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the reaction \( \text{K}^- + \text{p} \rightarrow \Lambda^0 + \pi^0 \), a charged K meson (mass \( 493.7 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) collides with a proton (\( 938.3 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) at rest, producing a lambda particle (\( 1115.7 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) and a neutral pi meson (\( 135.0 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)), as represented in figure. The initial kinetic energy of the K meson is \( 152.4 \text{ MeV} \). After the interaction, the pi meson has a kinetic energy of \( 254.8 \text{ MeV} \). Find the kinetic energy of the lambda.

the task is not direct lookup; it likely involves energy-conservation reasoning across initial and final states
multiple numerical values must be integrated to isolate the lambda kinetic energy
multiple-choice options are present, but the answer still depends on physics-based computation

the answer can be checked by conservation laws and arithmetic consistency
